## Disposition

| Review track | Status |
|---|---|
| **Absolute structural review** (4 SC-212-1 criteria, no OpenClaw comparison) | **4/4 PASS** |
| **Comparative side-by-side review** against OpenClaw reference | **BLOCKED — asset missing** |
| **T010 final disposition** | **PASS-with-deferred-side-by-side** |

The absolute structural review confirms the regenerated image satisfies all four SC-212-1 criteria as written. The side-by-side comparison against `openclaw-agent-threat-model-infographic.jpg` (whose visual style is the aspirational anchor) requires the asset on the local filesystem; the asset is not bundled with tachi and is not present in the repo or on the local Mac filesystem (verified via `find ~ -maxdepth 6 -iname 'openclaw-agent-threat-model-infographic.jpg'`). The comparative review is deferred to a follow-up session with a reviewer who has access to the asset (PM at the P1 checkpoint, post Wave 5, is the natural opportunity).

---

## SC-212-1 — Per-criterion absolute structural review

| # | Criterion | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Rounded-rectangle nodes (vs text-only) | **PASS** | Every component is rendered as a rounded-rectangle node with layer-coded fill (light blue for Supabase Platform, peach/orange for Application Layer, light green for External / Untrusted) and colored borders (red for high-severity nodes — MCP Server (HTTP), MCP Server (stdio), MCP Tool Surface, brain CLI). No text-only / bullet-list rendering — the failure mode the new prompt was meant to defeat. |
| 2 | Directional arrows with explicit arrowheads | **PASS** | Top-to-bottom directional arrows with explicit arrowheads visible at all five inter-layer transitions and at five intra-layer transitions: Supabase Platform → Application Layer; Ingest Edge Function → MCP Server (stdio); MCP Server (HTTP) → MCP Server (stdio); MCP Server (HTTP) → MCP Tool Surface; MCP Server (stdio) → MCP Tool Surface; Application Layer → External / Untrusted. Arrowheads are clearly drawn. |
| 3 | Leader-lined callouts on specific nodes | **PASS** | 5 callouts rendered with dashed leader-lines connecting each callout box to its referenced node: D-1 → MCP Server (HTTP); D-9 → MCP Tool Surface; AG-1 → MCP Server (stdio); AGP-01 → MCP Server (stdio); S-9 → brain CLI. Each callout box has a red border, severity icon (⚠), finding ID, severity tag (High), and description text. |
| 4 | ≥5 callouts | **PASS at minimum** | Exactly 5 callouts rendered (matches the T019 payload: 4 in Application Layer + 1 in External / Untrusted + 0 in Supabase Platform; total = 5; meets the ≥5 lower bound exactly). The "+ 4 more in this layer" annotation at the bottom of Application Layer correctly surfaces the residual qualifying findings per FR-212-9 `layer_overflow` semantics. |

### Bonus / Additional FR observations (beyond the 4 SC-212-1 criteria)

| Element | Result | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| FR-212-5 — Compact "0 H/C findings in this layer" badge for empty layers | **PASS** | Supabase Platform layer header includes a compact "0 High/Critical findings in this layer" badge directly under the layer title; well within ≤15% of layer height. |
| FR-212-9 — `layer_overflow` annotation rendered visually | **PASS** | "+ 4 more in this layer" appears as a compact in-layer annotation below Application Layer's component grid, surfacing the 4 residual qualifying findings that the per-layer ceiling pushed off the displayed callout list. |
| FR-212-4 — Per-layer border-accent colors from `visual-design-system.md` severity palette | **PASS** | High-severity component borders are deep red (`#DC2626`-family), matching the canonical severity palette established in `.claude/skills/tachi-infographics/references/visual-design-system.md`. |
| FR-212-7 — Preserved file output name + format + portrait orientation | **PASS** | Output is `threat-executive-architecture.jpg` (correct filename); JPEG format; 685×884 px (portrait, 1.29:1 aspect — consistent with the target rendering orientation). |
| Page-level title + footer | **PASS** | Title "Executive Threat Architecture: second-brain-mcp" at top; "Generated by Tachi Threat Modeling Framework — OWASP STRIDE + AI Threat Analysis" at bottom. |

---

## What the absolute review CANNOT establish

The absolute review cannot answer:

1. **Stylistic fidelity to OpenClaw** — does the regenerated image visually *feel like* the OpenClaw reference? Things like exact corner radius proportions, shadow depth, font choice, color saturation, leader-line dash density, callout box padding ratios are stylistic dimensions where "looks like OpenClaw" is the criterion. The new prompt instructs Gemini to render OpenClaw-style; the absolute review confirms structural compliance but cannot verify stylistic fidelity without the reference.

2. **Side-by-side per-element parity** — does each element type render with the same visual language as the OpenClaw reference (e.g., does the "compact empty-layer badge" look like OpenClaw's compact badges or like a different visual idiom)?

These are the dimensions a human reviewer with OpenClaw access would weigh in their own 4/4 score. The absolute review records that all 4 SC-212-1 criteria are met *as written* (the literal structural requirements), and the visual quality on individual structural axes is high (clean rendering, no obvious failure modes). The remaining qualitative judgment — "does it look like OpenClaw enough to ship?" — is for the human reviewer at the P1 checkpoint.

---

## Recommendation for the deferred comparative review (P1 checkpoint or sooner)

When the OpenClaw reference asset (`openclaw-agent-threat-model-infographic.jpg`) is available to a human reviewer (PM-equivalent or visual-design lead), they should:

1. Open `specs/212-improve-executive-architecture-infographic/artifacts/iteration-1/threat-executive-architecture.jpg` and `openclaw-agent-threat-model-infographic.jpg` side-by-side at full resolution.
2. Re-evaluate the 4 SC-212-1 criteria with comparative judgment, scoring each as PASS / FAIL.
3. If 4/4 PASS: the regenerated image ships under SC-212-1 unchanged. Update this review record with the comparative scores and reviewer identity/date.
4. If 3/4 PASS: invoke Risk R1 contingency (re-prioritize US3 ahead of US1 per `tasks.md` line 73; document the failing axis and either iterate the prompt OR accept the 3/4 image with a documented gap).
5. If <3/4 PASS after a 2nd iteration of T009: escalate to PM for scope decision per the original Risk R1 contingency.

The Risk R1 budget allows up to 3 iterations of T009 before escalation. iteration-1 is captured; iterations 2 and 3 remain available if the comparative review surfaces structural gaps.
